title: eriogonum parishii
intro: Eriogonum parishii, also known as Parish's buckwheat, is a perennial wildflower that belongs to the Polygonaceae family and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and fight infections. The health benefits of Eriogonum parishii include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable remedy for various health issues. Eriogonum parishii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used in traditional medicine to make poultices and compresses to apply directly to affected areas.
